meta.note_on_valuation_date: Each property's comparable age (age_days) and HPI-adjusted prices are computed against datetime.now() inside comparable_engine.py at the moment that property is fetched, NOT against a single frozen date for the whole run. See per-property 'fetch_timestamp' below. On a run this long, comparables can cross the 3-year (Direct) or 5-year (Development) recency cutoff between the first and last property tested — this is expected engine behaviour, not a bug in this validation script.

results.why_selected: Age-restricted retirement development — very distinct market segment from ordinary flats.
results.expected_challenge: Retirement/age-restricted leasehold flat; comparables likely confined to the same development (sparse, high-affinity Estate Evidence test).

results.property: Coral Springs Way, Richmond Villages, Witney
results.why_selected: Smallest/cheapest unit in the same retirement development as #21 — tests within-development price spread.
results.expected_challenge: 1-bed retirement leasehold flat; sparse comparables outside the development itself.

results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced below the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Converted mill building flat; tenure not stated on the listing — realistic data-gap case.
results.expected_challenge: Tenure unknown/unconfirmed at capture time; converted-building flat, floor area may be atypical.

results.why_selected: Established estate with multiple concurrent listings on the same road (see #26 too) — good same-estate density test.
results.expected_challenge: Estate/development affinity — several same-road listings should mutually reinforce Estate Evidence once sold.

results.why_selected: Adjacent close within the same wider Lady Grove estate as #24.
results.expected_challenge: Tests whether Estate Evidence correctly links closely-related but differently-named streets within one development.

results.why_selected: Newer-build estate, different part of Didcot from the Ladygrove/Lady Grove cluster.
results.expected_challenge: New-build-adjacent estate; existing hard gate excludes genuine new-build comparables, so evidence may be thinner than expected for a large estate.

results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Insufficient comparable evidence for a reliable opinion of value

results.why_selected: Shares a street name with existing property #20 (Ladygrove, Didcot) but is a distinct address/price point — tests whether the two are correctly NOT conflated.
results.expected_challenge: Same street name as an existing validation entry — false-duplication / affinity risk analogous to the Mill Street case (#16).
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Evidence is limited; asking price appears below the indicative range

results.why_selected: Unusually high bedroom count for a terraced house — likely a converted/extended property.
results.expected_challenge: Unusual layout — 7-bed terrace may be an HMO-style conversion; floor area and £/bedroom will be atypical vs standard 3-bed terrace comparables.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced modestly above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Dense inner-urban terraced street — expected to have plentiful genuine comparables, a useful contrast to the sparse-evidence cases in this set.
results.expected_challenge: Low expected challenge — control case for a well-evidenced urban terrace.
results.v1_recommendation_tagline: Priced modestly above the evidence-supported range
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced modestly above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Part of a block of apartments; bedroom count and tenure were not stated on the listing at capture time.
results.expected_challenge: Missing bedroom count and tenure — tests engine behaviour with incomplete subject-property data.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Entry-level flat, block-of-apartments listing with no bedroom count stated.
results.expected_challenge: Missing bedroom count; entry-level price band for the North West portfolio area.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Very low asking price for a 5-bed listing — plausible multi-unit/investment-block sale, not a typical owner-occupier flat.
results.expected_challenge: Price/bedroom mismatch suggests a non-standard transaction type; good stress test for whether the engine (or the not-yet-implemented PPD Category filter, ROADMAP item 4) would flag this as atypical.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Insufficient comparable evidence for a reliable opinion of value

results.why_selected: Extreme premium price point in a small market town centre — deliberately stresses the top end of the price range.
results.expected_challenge: Sparse comparables expected at this price point; town-centre premium road; genuinely obvious high-price outlier vs local market.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Village-edge new-ish estate near Banbury, multiple concurrent listings on the same close (see #35).
results.expected_challenge: Village location; estate/development affinity test alongside #35.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Insufficient comparable evidence for a reliable opinion of value

results.property: Arbor Park, Bodicote, Banbury (End of Terrace)
results.why_selected: Same close as #34 but smaller/cheaper — tests within-estate price differentiation.
results.expected_challenge: Same street as #34 — must NOT simply average the two together; genuinely different size/price point.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Insufficient comparable evidence for a reliable opinion of value

results.why_selected: Town-edge estate development, Banbury — geographic diversity away from the existing Didcot/Witney/Oxford cluster.
results.expected_challenge: Newer estate; genuine same-street comparables may be limited if the development is recent.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Agent's own listing explicitly flags 'extension potential' — direct test case for the extension-potential/planning module.
results.expected_challenge: Premium detached bungalow with agent-claimed extension potential; tests whether planning.py's scoring is directionally consistent with a real agent's own assessment. NOTE: original run (n=37 in the 2026-07-15 baseline CSV/JSON) produced an untrustworthy 0.0s/£0 result — see validation_baselines/property_37_reruns/README.md for the corrected isolated re-run (£199,700 V2, Medium confidence).
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Genuinely new UK region (South West, first Bristol entry in the dataset) and a CONFIRMED-tenure retirement leasehold flat — closes the confirmed-leasehold coverage gap flagged after the first batch.
results.expected_challenge: Retirement/age-restricted leasehold development; premium South West market with no existing comparable-region coverage in this dataset.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Premium Bristol (Clifton) flat — new region, high price band, tenure unconfirmed at capture (realistic data-gap case).
results.expected_challenge: Premium South West urban flat; tenure not stated on listing.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Genuinely new UK region (Yorkshire, first York entry) — new-build city-centre development, multiple concurrent listings on the same development (see #41, #43).
results.expected_challenge: New-build-adjacent development; Yorkshire region with no existing comparable-region coverage.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Same development as #40 but a different block (Kings, not Victoria) — tests within-development block-level differentiation.
results.expected_challenge: Same development as #40 — must not simply average the two blocks together.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Geographically distinct from the Hudson Quarter cluster (#40/#41/#43) within the same city — tests whether the engine correctly keeps genuinely separate York developments apart.
results.expected_challenge: Same city, different development — a within-region differentiation test rather than a cross-region one.
results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Priced above the evidence-supported range

results.why_selected: Third Hudson Quarter listing (Victoria block again, different unit/price than #40) — deepens the same-development comparable density for a York new-build case.
results.expected_challenge: Same development/block as #40 at a different price point — within-block price differentiation test.

results.v2_recommendation_tagline: Insufficient comparable evidence for a reliable opinion of value
